impact:
**Symptom:** On the fitting page (`/tools/fitting`), the right-hand stats panel sometimes shows raw JSON instead of stats, e.g.:

```
{"detail": "CSRF token invalid or missing — refresh the page and retry."}
```

The fit itself loads fine (modules in their slots, ship picker works), but stats are missing. Hit observed 2026-05-15 on a Babaroga fit named `*BABABOOEY`.

**Why it happens:** `recalcStats()` in `app/templates/fitting_tool.html` does:

```js
fetch('/tools/fitting/stats', { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, body: ... })
  .then(r => r.text())
  .then(html => { document.getElementById('stats-panel').innerHTML = html; });
```

It never checks `r.ok` / `r.status`. On any non-2xx response, the JSON error body gets stuffed into the panel as HTML. The CSRF middleware (`app/middleware/csrf.py`) returns 403 with a JSON body when the session's CSRF token doesn't match the X-CSRF-Token header.

**Why CSRF triggers here:** The page-level fetch wrapper in `base.html` auto-injects X-CSRF-Token from the `<meta name="csrf-token">` tag (line 6: `{{ request.session.get('csrf_token', '') }}`). That meta tag is filled at page-render time. If the session rotates the token mid-session (or if the page sits long enough for the session itself to time out), the meta tag becomes stale and the header doesn't match. The header IS being sent — it's just wrong.

**Fix (defensive, narrow):**
- In `recalcStats()`, check `response.ok` and `response.status`. On 403, render a friendly "session expired — refresh to retry" panel instead of `r.text()`. On 5xx, "server error, see logs".
- Consider applying the same pattern to the other `fetch()` callers in `fitting_tool.html` (there are several — ship search, module search, etc.).

**Fix (root-cause, broader):**
- The page-level fetch wrapper could re-read the meta tag on each call so it always uses the freshest token visible to the page — but that doesn't help if the session itself rotated server-side without the page knowing.
- A more durable fix: add a small "/csrf/refresh" endpoint that returns a fresh token, and have the wrapper retry once on 403 by fetching a new token + updating the meta tag.

**Workaround for now:** refresh the page. The fit is auto-saved to localStorage so nothing is lost.

**Out of scope:** the same brittleness probably affects other htmx-loaded panels (admin, structure timers, etc.), but htmx surfaces errors differently (it doesn't auto-innerHTML failed responses), so they're less likely to expose raw JSON. Worth a sweep but not in this ticket.</impact>
<parameter name="components">["fitting", "csrf"]
resolution: Fixed in commit 95698ce. Added _fitCheckOk() and _fitErrorHtml() helpers in app/templates/fitting_tool.html. recalcStats() now checks response.ok before rendering — on 403 shows "Session expired — refresh the page to continue. Your fit is saved locally", on other failures shows "Request failed (HTTP n)". Same defensive pattern applied to ship/module/drone search and exportEFT (so a JSON error doesn't get copied to clipboard).
title: Fitting stats panel renders raw JSON error on CSRF/auth failure
